{{Short description|Force per unit area applied in a direction perpendicular to a surface}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Pressure
| image = Pressure force area.svg
| caption = Pressure is the force applied perpendicular to a surface per unit area.
| topics = [[Force]], [[Thermodynamics]], [[Fluid dynamics]], [[Kinetic theory of gases]]
| symbol = p, P
| SI_unit = [[pascal (unit)|pascal]] (Pa)
| dimension = M L⁻¹ T⁻²
}}

'''Pressure''' (symbol <math>p</math> or <math>P</math>) is the [[force]] applied perpendicular to the surface of an object per unit [[area]] over which that force is distributed. It is a [[scalar (physics)|scalar]] quantity.

:<math>p = \frac{F_\perp}{A}</math>

where <math>F_\perp</math> is the normal force and <math>A</math> is the area.

The [[SI unit]] of pressure is the [[pascal (unit)|pascal]] (Pa), equal to one [[newton (unit)|newton]] per square metre (N/m²). Other common units include the [[atmosphere (unit)|atmosphere]] (atm), [[bar (unit)|bar]], [[torr]], and [[pounds per square inch]] (psi).

== Types ==

=== Hydrostatic pressure ===
In a static fluid at rest, the pressure at depth <math>h</math> is:
:<math>p = p_0 + \rho gh</math>
where <math>p_0</math> is the surface pressure, <math>\rho</math> is the fluid density, and <math>g</math> is the [[gravitational acceleration]].

=== Gas pressure ===
In the [[kinetic theory of gases]], pressure arises from molecular collisions with container walls:
:<math>p = \frac{1}{3}\rho\langle v^2 \rangle = \frac{2}{3}\frac{N}{V}\langle E_k \rangle</math>

where <math>\langle v^2 \rangle</math> is the mean square speed of the molecules.

=== Dynamic pressure ===
In flowing fluids, [[Bernoulli's principle]] relates static pressure to dynamic pressure:
:<math>p + \frac{1}{2}\rho v^2 + \rho gh = \text{constant}</math>

=== Atmospheric pressure ===
Standard atmospheric pressure at sea level is:
:1 atm = 101,325 Pa = 760 mmHg (torr) = 14.696 psi

== Pressure in thermodynamics ==
{{Main|Laws of thermodynamics}}
In [[thermodynamics]], pressure is a fundamental [[state variable]]. For an [[ideal gas]], the relationship between pressure, volume, temperature, and amount of substance is given by the [[ideal gas law]]:

:<math>pV = nRT = Nk_B T</math>

== Measurement ==
Pressure is measured using devices such as:
* [[Barometer]] — measures atmospheric pressure
* [[Manometer]] — measures gas pressure
* [[Bourdon gauge]] — mechanical pressure measurement
* [[Piezoelectric sensor]] — electronic pressure measurement

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Buoyancy]]
* [[Fluid dynamics]]
* [[Force]]
* [[Ideal gas law]]
* [[Kinetic theory of gases]]
* [[Pascal (unit)]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Pressure| ]]
[[Category:Fluid dynamics]]
[[Category:Thermodynamics]]
[[Category:Physical quantities]]
